strands.title: Task Structure and Scope
strands.content: A comprehensive code review of the pichay codebase is requested across exactly 7 source files (proxy.py, pager.py, eval.py, replay.py, analyzer.py, trimmer.py, __main__.py) in src/pichay/ and test files in tests/. The review must assess three dimensions for source files (code quality, potential bugs/edge cases, cross-module consistency) and two dimensions for test files (coverage gaps, false-pass risks). Output must be specific with line numbers and file paths, formatted to review.md in project root. This is a READ-and-ASSESS task, not refactor. The task has now entered execution phase: prior cycle was meta-level parsing; current cycle attempts file I/O. The assistant has signaled readiness to read files in parallel but has not yet provided file contents—indicating either in-progress execution or awaiting environment capability confirmation.

key_claims.text: The review scope includes exactly 7 source files and test files in tests/
The review must produce findings with specific line numbers and file paths
Five distinct assessment dimensions are required (code quality, bugs/edge cases, consistency, coverage, false passes)
Execution phase has begun but file contents have not yet been retrieved and integrated

strands.title: Assessment Dimensions and Criteria
strands.content: The review evaluates source code across five dimensions: (1) Code quality—unused imports, dead code, type errors, style inconsistencies; (2) Potential bugs and unhandled edge cases—logical errors, boundary conditions, missing error handling; (3) Cross-module consistency—naming conventions, error handling patterns, API contracts between modules. For tests: (4) Missing coverage for important code paths—untested branches, unexercised error handlers; (5) False-positive risks—tests asserting incorrect behavior, mocking that masks bugs, insufficient assertions. These dimensions span both surface-level hygiene and deep logical soundness. The criteria remain unchanged from cycle 1 but are now ready to be applied against actual code content once files are read.

key_claims.text: Code quality assessment includes unused imports, dead code, type errors, and style issues
Bug/edge-case assessment focuses on logical errors, boundary conditions, and error handling completeness
Cross-module consistency assessment spans naming conventions, error patterns, and API contracts
Test review must identify both coverage gaps and false-positive risks from weak assertions or mocking

strands.title: Execution Environment and File I/O Status
strands.content: The second cycle begins the actual file reading phase. The assistant has stated readiness to 'read all source files and test files in parallel' but has not yet provided file contents or confirmation of successful reads. This signals either: (a) files are being fetched asynchronously; (b) file access is pending confirmation; (c) the assistant requires explicit invocation to begin file reads. The execution model is unclear—whether files will be provided in the next message, or whether the assistant needs explicit permission/capability to read. No errors have been reported, suggesting no immediate I/O failures. Dependency chain: file reads must complete before analysis; analysis must complete before review writing.

key_claims.text: File I/O is now active—files are being read or about to be read from src/pichay/ and tests/
No file contents have been integrated into the tensor yet as of cycle 2
The execution model permits parallel file reads without blocking analysis

declared_losses.what_was_lost: No actual code content has been retrieved or analyzed; cycle 2 message signals intent but provides no file data
declared_losses.why: File I/O has not been completed or reported. Analysis cannot proceed without actual code.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Context about pichay's purpose, architecture, and design intent—still unknown
declared_losses.why: File reading has not yielded domain information. This context remains necessary for distinguishing intentional design from bugs.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed clarification of what constitutes 'code quality' in this specific project
declared_losses.why: No project standards, linting configuration, or type-checking setup has been disclosed. Generic criteria will be applied, but project-specific standards remain unestablished.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Explicit confirmation that all 7 files exist and are accessible
declared_losses.why: No file list or directory tree has been provided. It's unknown whether all expected files are present.

open_questions: Have all 7 source files (proxy.py, pager.py, eval.py, replay.py, analyzer.py, trimmer.py, __main__.py) been successfully read from src/pichay/?
Have all test files in tests/ been identified and read?
What is the actual Python version, framework dependencies, and architecture of pichay?
Does pichay have established project standards (linting rules, type hints, docstring requirements)?
Are there existing CI/CD or testing configurations that indicate coverage expectations?
Will file contents be provided in the next cycle, or does the system require explicit file access capability?
What is the intended scope of edge-case analysis—all theoretical inputs, or only documented/realistic scenarios?
instructions_for_next: NEXT CYCLE: (1) CRITICAL—Provide or retrieve actual file contents for all 7 source files. If files are inaccessible, report immediately. (2) For each source file, extract: imports, function/class definitions, signatures, control flow, error handling, return patterns. Flag obvious code smells (unused variables, uncaught exceptions, missing error handling). (3) Read all test files. Extract: test names, what they test, assertion patterns, mocking, obvious gaps (untested modules, unexercised branches). (4) Collect specific evidence with line numbers for each of the 5 assessment dimensions. (5) Map cross-module dependencies to assess consistency in naming, error contracts, and API patterns. (6) Synthesize findings into severity tiers: CRITICAL (logic error, unhandled exception), MAJOR (design inconsistency, coverage gap), MINOR (hygiene, style). (7) Generate review.md with structured sections: Source Code Findings, Test Analysis, Consistency Issues, Recommendations. Do NOT output review.md yet—first integrate file analysis into the tensor.
